column1: # comment: "Accessibility alignments \u2014 schema.org, WHO ICF, IndoorGML/IMDF, and SOSA."

column7: sosa:Observation is the generic observation class; purrdf:AccessibilityAssertion is a specialised gufo:Relator for accessibility claims. Both carry provenance and result.
ICF b2 covers sensory functions including hearing.
ICF e125 covers assistive products for communication including hearing aids and induction loops.
ICF d4 (Mobility) encompasses the spatial requirements for physical clearance.
ICF b1 covers mental functions including cognitive processes.
ICF e140 covers assistive products for learning and education, relevant to cognitive access.
ICF e5 covers services, systems and policies including health and medical services relevant to life-support access.
ICF b2 covers sensory functions including vision.
ICF e135 covers assistive products for communication including visual aids.
ICF d4 (Mobility) encompasses wheelchair and step-free locomotion.

column4: Products and technology for personal mobility
column7: ICF e115 covers assistive products for personal mobility (wheelchairs, walkers, ramps).

schema:accessibilityFeature is a flat textual enum on Place/CreativeWork; PURRDF hasAccessibilityFeature is an object property ranging over AccessibilityFacet individuals. Directional lossy projection.
schema:accessibilityHazard is a flat textual enum for hazards; PURRDF hasBarrier is an object property ranging over AccessibilityFacet individuals. Directional lossy projection.
